Welcome aboard. Now don't touch anything and I'll take you through the instrumentation you see before you.
This is the Threat Circle. That notch indicates North. As for its other functions, I'll demonstrate them later.
This is your energy level. Energy is used to power the shields in Walker mode or enable you to fly in Jet mode. It will regenerate whilst you're on the ground.
This is your armour level. As Aquila takes damage, the protective plating gets destroyed. If this gauge reaches zero then you're in big trouble.
Here is your weapon indicator. This shows you what weapon is currently selected. It also allows you to keep track of its temperature or remaining ammo level.
The circle to the left is your Scanner. Enemy units show up in red, friendly units in blue.
The circle to the right is the Battle Line indicator. Blue areas are under friendly control and red areas are held by the enemy.
Objectives show up as yellow dots on both the Scanner and Battle Line indicator.
Okay, Hawk? I want you to manoeuvre the Battle Engine to the area marked on your HUD.
Notice how Objectives that you are given are marked as yellow dots on your scanner.
Now make your way to the firing range for target practice. I'll mark the location on your HUD again.
In a moment we're going to activate your weapon systems.
The IS-5 Pulse Cannon is your primary weapon. It can be fired rapidly or charged up to release a larger round.
As an energy weapon it never runs out of ammo but it can overheat. It is most effective against enemy vehicles or buildings.
Aquila is also equipped with a rapid-fire Vulcan Cannon. It has limited target tracking facilities, so it's really easy to use.
You have limited ammo with this one and it's only really effective against infantry or lightly armoured units.
Now destroy the three tanks and that building marked on your HUD.
There are 3 Trucks nearby. Try the Vulcan Cannon on them.
Aquila can also perform emergency dodge manoeuvres. Rapidly moving the stick left then right will cause her to dash right.
That fighter is going to attack. Try using those moves to avoid these incoming missiles.
Incoming missiles show up as triangles on the Threat Circle. Triangles on the left of the circle are missiles to your left and so on.
Nice moves. That should help you a lot in a combat situation.
Hmmm. Not to worry; I'm sure you'll pick it up in no time.
Now I want you to engage Jet mode and land on that island to the South. Aquila isn't designed for aquatic activities so be careful.
To land safely, transform back into Walker mode over the target and use your retro thrusters to touch down softly.
Now let's see how you do against a few moving targets. Track them down using your scanner. Pick your targets carefully and don't damage the surrounding buildings.
Aquila is also fitted with a zoom control. You can use this to get a closer view of targets at distance.
We're now going to send over some remote drones. Take them out of the air in Jet mode. Need I remind you about the water?
In Jet mode, your left stick controls your throttle. Push forwards to accelerate and back to brake.
You might find these next drones a little more tricky; we've activated their weapon systems.
In a combat situation, making Aquila strafe sideways makes you a considerably harder target to hit.
Excellent work. I think we're done for now. Bring Aquila back here; we've got some diagnostics to run.
This is a Repair Pad. If ever you need repairs or ammunition, simply stand Aquila on the top of this pad and wait.
Need a hand with those drones? We're activating the base defences.
Okay, I guess that was a bit much for you. Bring Aquila back here; we've got some diagnostics to run.
Not a bad little run for your first time out. What can I say? You're a natural. Now get some rest; tomorrow's a long day.
Well, you've still got some things to learn, but that's enough for today. Get some rest, Hawk.
Nicely done. Bring Aquila to this location for the next section.
Your left stick controls the direction of travel and the right dictates which way Aquila faces.
I don't think Kramer's going to be too happy, Hawk.
Get that good for nothing punk out of my Battle Engine before he kills someone!
Check your targets, rookie! I don't want any damage done to these facilities.
All systems nominal
Weapon Temperature critical. Safeguards in place.
Ammunition depleted.
Mobility systems activated.
Weapon systems activated. Master arm on.
When your weapon overheats, let it cool down before firing again.
If you run out of ammo in combat, you'll be in trouble. Try to take it easy!
Watch it! That thing's not designed for water.
